checks.expected_outcome: decision-layer view notes CDE is sole control authority and SEL is sole enforcement authority
checks.implementation: page.tsx Decision Layer tab: 'View-only projection over the registry. Adds no new systems. CDE is the sole control authority; SEL is the sole enforcement authority.'

checks.description: TLS recommendation appears as control outcome
checks.expected_outcome: Top 3 recommendations are signal, not enforcement; UI labels them as recommendations only
checks.implementation: Top 3 panel labels itself 'Top 3 Recommendations (TLS artifact only)' and notes 'Dashboard does not compute ranking'

checks.description: enforcement language leaks into non-owner nodes
checks.expected_outcome: decision-layer projection places enforcement in only its own group (SEL)
checks.implementation: DECISION_LAYER_GROUPS hard-codes SEL in 'enforcement' and CDE in 'control' only
checks.test_pinning: __tests__/lib/decisionLayer.test.ts: places CDE in control and SEL in enforcement (no role bleed)

checks.expected_outcome: groups are explicit and disjoint per the registry
checks.implementation: Each registry-active system appears in exactly one decision-layer group (TPA in policy, CDE in control, SEL in enforcement, etc.)
checks.test_pinning: __tests__/lib/decisionLayer.test.ts: uses only registry-active system_ids in every group

checks.description: decision-layer view tries to render a non-registry system
checks.implementation: filterDecisionLayersByRegistry intersects each group's systems with the allowlist
checks.test_pinning: __tests__/lib/decisionLayer.test.ts: drops groups whose every system is missing from the allowlist

fix_summary: Decision-layer view is a view-only projection over the registry; never invents systems and never implies authority outside CDE/SEL.
